question: Looking at Figure 1.2-6, how does the cutaway view of the NPM show the relative sizes of the containment and reactor vessels?

nuscale_ch01_0002.title: Introduction
nuscale_ch01_0002.summary: This section introduces the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR), which is required for standard design certification under U.S. nuclear regulations. It outlines the scope of the report, describing the NuScale Power Module (NPM) as a modular, scalable nuclear steam supply system, and highlights key safety and design features such as passive safety systems, compact containment, and advanced digital controls.

nuscale_ch01_0051.title: Plant Features of Special Interest
nuscale_ch01_0051.summary: This section describes key plant features of special interest in the NuScale Power Plant, including the Central Utility Building (CUB) and Diesel Generator Buildings (DGB), which house essential but nonsafety-related systems and backup power equipment. It also highlights human factors considerations, emphasizing fail-safe design, automation, digital controls, and workload management to minimize human error and meet regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0036.title: Refueling Operations
nuscale_ch01_0036.summary: Refueling operations for a NuScale Power Module (NPM) are conducted independently from other modules. The process involves moving the NPM from its operating bay to the refueling pool, disassembling it for inspection, maintenance, and refueling, and then reassembling and returning it to service after successful testing and startup procedures.

nuscale_ch01_0001.title: CHAPTER 1 INTRODUCTION AND GENERAL DESCRIPTION OF THE PLANT
nuscale_ch01_0001.summary: This section introduces the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR), which is required for standard design certification under U.S. regulations. It provides an overview of the NuScale Power Module (NPM), a scalable, modular nuclear steam supply system designed for flexible deployment, highlighting its key safety features, compact design, and advanced instrumentation. The FSAR covers both individual module and multi-module plant configurations, focusing on safety analysis and operational limits.

nuscale_ch01_0035.title: Fuel Handling and Reactor Maintenance Areas
nuscale_ch01_0035.summary: The fuel handling and reactor maintenance areas are located at the west end of the Reactor Building and include spaces for the spent fuel pool, refueling pool, and dry dock, all designed to support safe fuel handling and reactor maintenance operations. These areas facilitate the movement and storage of new and spent fuel assemblies, provide radiation protection for workers, and are equipped with cranes and access points for efficient operation during refueling and maintenance activities.

nuscale_ch01_0005.title: Reactor Type
nuscale_ch01_0005.summary: The NuScale reactor is a small modular pressurized water reactor (PWR) featuring a passive safety design. Each module integrates the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel, surrounded by a steel containment vessel designed to withstand high-pressure events. The plant can include up to 12 modules, each producing 160 MWt (about 50 MWe), for a total net output of approximately 570 MWe for a 12-module facility.

nuscale_ch01_0015.title: General Plant Description
nuscale_ch01_0015.summary: This section provides a summary of the NuScale plant’s overall design, including its main systems such as safety features, power conversion, fuel handling, cooling, and waste management. It explains the distinction between certified and site-specific design elements, clarifies the use of representative information for context, and notes how security-related details are handled. Each COL applicant must reference the NuScale FSAR and provide site-specific information as required.

nuscale_ch01_0007.title: Schedule
nuscale_ch01_0007.summary: The NuScale containment vessel is a cylindrical, steel, ASME-certified pressure vessel designed to withstand high-pressure events and operates under vacuum during normal conditions. The reactor is a passive, integral small modular pressurized water reactor (SMR) with natural circulation and no need for external piping or coolant pumps. Each NuScale Power Module (NPM) produces 160 MWt (about 50 MWe), and a 12-module plant yields a net output of approximately 570 MWe; applicants referencing this design must provide their own construction and operation schedules.

nuscale_ch01_0168.title: SRP 6.5.3, Rev 3: Fission Product Control Systems and StructuresII.2 Secondary Containment
nuscale_ch01_0168.summary: The NuScale design does not include secondary containment or fission product clean-up systems, as its passive safety features and submerged containment vessel inherently control fission products. Therefore, regulatory criteria related to secondary containment and certain fission product control systems are not applicable to the NuScale Power Module, and only specific containment parameters such as leakage rates are relevant for review.

nuscale_ch01_0167.title: SRP 6.5.3, Rev 3: Fission Product Control Systems and StructuresII.1 Primary Containment
nuscale_ch01_0167.summary: The NuScale design partially conforms to SRP 6.5.3 requirements for primary containment, as its passive containment vessel does not include fission product clean-up or pressure suppression systems; instead, fission product control is achieved through inherent design features. Requirements related to secondary containment and other fission product control systems are not applicable, as the NuScale design does not include these features. Only the containment vessel, isolation valves, and passive barriers are credited for fission product control in the NuScale Power Plant.

nuscale_ch01_0161.title: SRP 6.4, Rev 3: Control Room Habitability SystemII.5 Relative Location of Source and Control Room
nuscale_ch01_0161.summary: This section explains that certain regulatory criteria related to control room habitability and emergency ventilation systems do not apply to the NuScale reactor design, as it uses compressed air tanks for clean air during accidents, eliminating reliance on ventilation system inlets. Dose guidelines for new reactors are partially applicable, while toxic gas hazard requirements are the responsibility of the COL applicant. Additionally, NuScale does not use engineered safety feature (ESF) filter or ventilation systems for accident mitigation, so related guidance is not applicable.

nuscale_ch01_0073.title: Departures
nuscale_ch01_0073.summary: This section outlines the interface requirements between the NuScale Power Plant’s certified design and the site-specific design provided in the combined license (COL) application. It explains the types of interface requirements (CDI and COL), lists the information items that COL applicants must address, and specifies that any departures from the certified design must be documented by the COL applicant.

nuscale_ch01_0033.title: General Arrangement of Major Structures and Equipment
nuscale_ch01_0033.summary: This section describes the general arrangement of major structures and equipment at the NuScale Power Plant, focusing on the centrally located Reactor Building (RXB). The RXB is a large, reinforced concrete structure designed for safety and radiation protection, housing the NuScale Power Modules (NPMs) in a common reactor pool with individual bays arranged in two rows. The section also references detailed figures and notes that some buildings, like the administration and warehouse, are outside the report's scope.

nuscale_ch01_0390.title: SRP 9.5.1.1, Rev 0: Fire Protection ProgramII.7 Operational Program and Proposed Implementation Milestones
nuscale_ch01_0390.summary: This section explains that the operational fire protection program and its implementation milestones are the responsibility of the Combined License (COL) applicant, not the NuScale design certification applicant. NuScale acknowledges the relevant regulatory guidance but notes that full compliance, especially regarding passive separation of redundant trains, may not always be feasible due to the modular design. Therefore, NuScale ensures that fire protection measures are in place to maintain shutdown capability, while ultimate program implementation is deferred to the COL applicant.

nuscale_ch01_0060.title: NuScale Integral System Test Program
nuscale_ch01_0060.summary: The NuScale Integral System Test Program was established to collect thermal-hydraulic data for system characterization and to validate safety analysis codes using a scaled model of the NuScale Power Module (NPM). The NIST-1 facility replicates key reactor systems and supports the development and validation of proprietary safety codes, ensuring accurate modeling of the NPM’s unique features. Testing at NIST-1 has provided essential data for NuScale’s safety analysis and design certification efforts.

nuscale_ch01_0039.title: Technical Support Center
nuscale_ch01_0039.summary: The Technical Support Center (TSC) is established in compliance with NUREG-0696 requirements to support plant operations, with further details provided in Section 13.3. The TSC, along with advanced digital control systems and specialized monitoring displays in the main control room, ensures effective supervision, diagnostics, and response for all reactor modules and plant systems.
